issue_title: Deployment artifacts for Quarkus extensions are not in deployment dir
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

As stated in Quarkus docs[1], when using `mutable-jar`(default in Keycloak) the deployment JARs and their dependencies need to be in `/lib/deployment` folder. However, in the latest main, they're part of `lib/main` alongside runtime dependencies - which is wrong. 

Users are able to remove deployment JARs for optimized images by removing the `/lib/deployment` folder[2], which is not possible now. 

For 26.0.8, JARs are properly located in `/lib/deployment`.

Not totally sure what other problems it might bring in terms of Quarkus class loaders, so it should be fixed for the next release.

[1] https://quarkus.io/guides/reaugmentation
[2] https://quarkus.io/guides/reaugmentation#3-optional-delete-the-deployments-folder

### Version

main

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Deployment JARs for Quarkus extensions are in `/lib/deployment` folder.

### Actual behavior

Deployment JARs for Quarkus extensions are in `/lib/main` folder.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Execute `./mvnw clean install -f quarkus/server/pom.xml`
2. Check the `quarkus/server/target/lib` folder.

### Anything else?

_No response_

Deployment artifacts for Quarkus extensions are not in deployment dir (#36461)

issue_title: DPoP: Refresh token created with DPoP can be refreshed without proof
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

* When I enable the DPoP feature, I can create a DPoP refresh token on any client, even if I don't enable "OAuth 2.0 DPoP Bound Access Tokens Enabled" for that client. 
* For a DPoP bound refresh token, I can then refresh it without DPoP proof for a non-DPoP bound refresh and access token as long as "OAuth 2.0 DPoP Bound Access Tokens Enabled" is not enabled, even though the spec says both the access and the refresh token should be bound to the DPoP key. 

From a security point of view, this might be surprising: Assuminging someone enabled the DPoP feature, and didn't switch on the flag on the client level and then issued themselves a DPoP and a refresh token created with DPoP. Now they can still use the refresh token without a DPoP proof, which is against the spec. 


### Version

26.1

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

* Allow clients to opt in to use DPoP if the option is off, as it allows experimenting with DPoP and gradually rolling out DPoP
* Option in the client config should be named "Require Demonstrating Proof of Possession (DPoP) header in token requests" (similar to Okta's configuation in https://developer.okta.com/docs/guides/dpop/nonoktaresourceserver/main/) or "Only issue DPoP bound access and refresh tokens" (which would take the perspective of Keycloak, and the help could reference the DPoP spec and the header requirements) 
* If a refresh token has been issued with DPoP, the next refresh needs to use enforce DPoP

### Actual behavior

Already works as expected:
* Allow clients to opt in to use DPoP if the option is off, as it allows experimenting with DPoP and gradually rolling out DPoP

Actual behavior (not yet as expected): 
* Option in the client config is named "OAuth 2.0 DPoP Bound Access Tokens Enabled"
* If a refresh token has been issued with DPoP, the next refresh DOES NOT enforce DPoP, and the generated access tokens and refresh tokens are not bound to DPoP

### How to Reproduce?

* Start Keycloak with the DPoP feature enabled
* Create a public client that is not enforcing DPoP 
* Use a DPoP client (like https://github.com/panva/openid-client) to create a DPoP bound access and refresh token
* Then refresh the token without a DPoP headers -> this works, and the access and refresh tokens issued can be used without a DPoP header



### Anything else?

As discussed on the Keycloak security mailing list, this is considered a hardening issue. 

Comment from @mposolda:

> - Agree with your suggestion to rename the client option to something like "Enforce/require" instead of "Enable" . IMO we can rename the labels in the UI and java methods on OIDCAdvancedConfigWrapper, but still keep the name of the client attribute (to avoid issues with migration/backwards compatibility)
> - IMO when the DPoP feature is enabled, we can still allow clients without enforcing DPoP (so with the switch above OFF) to issue DPoP refresh tokens and access tokens. In that case, when DPoP tokens were used, it can be ideal if we also enforce DPoP at token verification. We can take a look at the claims in the refresh token (or access token) and if DPoP claims are present, we should verify DPoP. This will allow clients to migrate/test DPoP without enforcing it, which is possibly aligned with your suggestion? It can be also good to doublecheck the specification in this regards. I am just not 100% sure if we can reliably detect DPoP used by claims in the tokens as standard claims like "cnf" are not DPoP specific. For access tokens, we probably can due the token type "dpop", but not sure about refresh tokens. Maybe if needed, we can add some custom claim into tokens to detect that it is DPoP refresh token, not sure....

Comment from me: 

* For the access tokens, we already have have in the tokens "typ" set to "DPoP". I didn't find that in the standard
* The refresh tokens are AFAIK opaque, so it might be ok to use the "cnf" if we don't use it in other places.
commit_message:
DPoP: Refresh token created with DPoP can be refreshed without proof

closes #36475

Signed-off-by: Takashi Norimatsu <takashi.norimatsu.ws@hitachi.com>

issue_title: DPoP: User Info Endpoint authorization type mismatch
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

When testing DPoP, I found that Keycloak's user info endpoint accepts a regular access token with both "Authorization: DPoP ..." and "Authorization: Bearer ...".
Looking at the code, this seems to be limited to the user info endpoint. 

I tested this with KC 26.0.7 and main. It works even if the DPoP feature is not enabled. 


### Version

26.1

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

* An "Authorization: DPoP ..." header should only work when the DPoP feature is enabled.
* An "Authorization: Bearer ..." should never accept an access token issued for DPoP.
* Keycloak should show an error when more than one "Authorization: ... " header is presented by the client. See https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9449#section-7.2 for the requirement

Implementation note: Currently we first extract, then check the token, and finally check the DPoP proof. Given the current structure of the code, maybe it might be good to move the validation around DPoP to when we extract the token from the HTTP header, so we don't lose the information about "Bearer" vs. "DPoP". 



### Actual behavior

* An "Authorization: DPoP ..." header works all the time, even if DPoP is not enabled
* An "Authorization: Bearer ..." accepts a DPoP issued token as long as it is accompanied with the DPoP proof header
* Keycloak picks only the first authorization header


### How to Reproduce?

* Start up Keycloak with DPoP disabled
* Create an access token and send it to the user info endpoint with "Authorization: DPoP ..." -> KC will accept it

### Anything else?

This has been discussed on the Keycloak security mailing list and was considered a hardening issue. 

Implementation note: Currently we first extract, then check the token, and finally check the DPoP proof. Given the current structure of the code, maybe it might be good to move the validation around DPoP to when we extract the token from the HTTP header, so we don't lose the information about "Bearer" vs. "DPoP". 

Comment from @mposolda:

> Agree with your suggestions to allow DPoP just when DPoP feature is enabled. Also to reject the request if more "Authorization" headers are used (I believe this is aligned with the specification [1]). Also +1 to your suggestion regarding "implementation note"
commit_message:
DPoP: User Info Endpoint authorization type mismatch

closes #36476

Signed-off-by: Takashi Norimatsu <takashi.norimatsu.ws@hitachi.com>

issue_title: Duplicated code due to typo in DefaultHttpClientFactory
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

_No response_

### Describe the bug

Commit https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/commit/c3a15cb3 introduced support to get the reuse connections config property from a sys env.
However, the configuration value was assigned to a variable with a minor spelling error.

The new variable was used to set the `reuseConnections` property of the `HttpClientBuilder`, but the property is also set a few lines up in the same code block.

This should be cleaned up to avoid later errors or confusion.

Related code:
```java
private void lazyInit(KeycloakSession session) {
    ...
    boolean reuseConnections = config.getBoolean("reuse-connections", true);
    ...
    boolean resuseConnections = getBooleanConfigWithSysPropFallback("reuse-connections", true);
    ...
    HttpClientBuilder builder = newHttpClientBuilder();
    builder.socketTimeout(socketTimeout, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS)
            ...
            .reuseConnections(reuseConnections)
            ...
            .reuseConnections(resuseConnections);
```

### Version

26.1.0

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

No code cuplication within the same block

### Actual behavior

...

### How to Reproduce?

...

### Anything else?

Related to https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/36464
commit_message:
Avoid reuseConnections config to be set twice in DefaultHttpClientFactory by removing duplicate code (#36489)

Closes #36498

issue_title: Viewing user events requires `view-realm`-role
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

With 26.1.0 a new tab was added to the user details page in the admin console, allowing to view all events related to the open user.

To view this tab, a user has to have the role `view-realm`. Otherwise it just doesn't exist.
This seems to be a mistake. To view the entire events list, a user needs the role `view-events`. I think if a user has this role, he should also have this new tab.

### Version

26.1.0

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

A user in the admin console should see a events tab in the user details page if he has the role `view-events`.

### Actual behavior

A user can only see this tab if he has the role `view-realm`.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Get a Keycloak instance
2. Create a user and give him the role `view-events`
3. Log into the admin console as this user, open a user detail page and notice the events tab missing
4. Remove the `view-events` role of that user and replace it with the `view-realm` role.
5. Log into the admin console as this user, open a user detail page and notice the events tab being shown

### Anything else?

_No response_

Keycloak can only be built with JDK 17 or JDK 21

As experienced in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/36541,
it fails to build in various ways if you try to
use JDK 23.

Also add instructions on how to specify JDK version when building
(helpful when you have multiple JDK versions installed).

Closes #36541

issue_title: Policy enforcer do not handle suppressed server resources
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authorization-services

### Describe the bug

In some scenarios, a resource may have been removed of the KC server but still exists in the path cache of the policy enforcer. Consequently, the policy enforcer cannot obtain a permission token from the KC server for these deleted resources and throw a runtime error.

### Version

26.0.7

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The policy enforcer should evict/update resources from the cache.

### Actual behavior

Permission request for deleted resources throw a runtime error from the policy enforcer

### How to Reproduce?

1. Configure an service with authz + policy enforcer + cache enabled
2. Ask for permission to access to a protected resource and obtain a permission ticket
3. Remove the protected resource in server
4. Ask again for permission to the service to access to the deleted protected resource => 500 error

` Error creating permission ticket
2025-01-16T10:43:57 	at org.keycloak.authorization.client.util.Throwables.retryAndWrapExceptionIfNecessary(Throwables.java:91) ~[keycloak-authz-client-26.0.3.jar:26.0.3]
2025-01-16T10:43:57 	at org.keycloak.authorization.client.resource.PermissionResource.create(PermissionResource.java:121) ~[keycloak-authz-client-26.0.3.jar:26.0.3]
2025-01-16T10:43:57 	at org.keycloak.authorization.client.resource.PermissionResource.create(PermissionResource.java:96) ~[keycloak-authz-client-26.0.3.jar:26.0.3]
2025-01-16T10:43:57 	at org.keycloak.adapters.authorization.PolicyEnforcer.getPermissionTicket(PolicyEnforcer.java:564) ~[keycloak-policy-enforcer-26.0.3.jar:26.0.3]
2025-01-16T10:43:57 	at org.keycloak.adapters.authorization.PolicyEnforcer.challenge(PolicyEnforcer.java:337) ~[keycloak-policy-enforcer-26.0.3.jar:26.0.3]`

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Add new error type in authz client and handle properly missing resources errors to enable policy enforcer resource cache eviction/update.

Closes #36560

Signed-off-by: Emilien Bondu <dev.ebondu@gmail.com>

issue_title: Organization invite link leads to non-defined page, when clicked second time
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

organizations

### Describe the bug

When a user has already joined an organization, clicking the organization invitation link the second time leads to a page with missing texts, and finally into the account management page, instead of organizations home page.

### Version

26.1.0

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When attempting to join the same organization the second time
* the dialog texts should be defined
* the link should lead you into the Organization's 'Redirect URL'

### Actual behavior

* the dialog texts are undefined
* the link leads you to the account management page

### How to Reproduce?

* Create a user
* Create an organization
* Send an email invitation link using the email address of the user you just created

* Click the invitation link you received -> a page with dialog 'You are about to join organization ***' opens, and the 'Click here to proceed' leads to login page, and finally into the application defined as the 'Redirect URL' of the Organization in question

* Click the same link again -> a page with dialog 'orgMemberAlready orgMemberAlready' opens. The 'Back to application' link leads to the 'account' page, instead of the 'Redirect URL' of the Organization in question

### Anything else?

_No response_

Organization invite link leads to non-defined page, when clicked second time

Closes #36569

issue_title: [Admin API] briefRepresentation behavior is wrong on OrganizationsResource.search
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

When searching organizations with Admin API, the behavior of briefRepresentation is wrong.

### Version

26.1.0

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When using `briefRepresentation=true` (which should be the default behavior), attributes should not be returned.
When using `briefRepresentation=false` (which should not be the default behavior), attributes should be returned.




### Actual behavior

When using `briefRepresentation=true` attributes are returned

```
curl https://mykeycloak.com/admin/realms/myrealm/organizations\?q\=my-id:1&briefRepresentation\=true -H "Authorization: Bearer $token"
[{"id":"5d4b9102-125f-4b00-955c-472ca12b88e9","name":"realms","alias":"realms","enabled":true,"description":"","attributes":{"my-id":["1"],"emailOtp":["true"]},"domains":[{"name":"mykeycloak.com","verified":false}]}]
```

When using `briefRepresentation=false` attributes are not returned

```
curl https://mykeycloak.com/admin/realms/myrealm/organizations\?q\=my-id:1&briefRepresentation\=true -H "Authorization: Bearer $token"
[{"id":"5d4b9102-125f-4b00-955c-472ca12b88e9","name":"realms","alias":"realms","enabled":true,"description":"","domains":[{"name":"mykeycloak.com","verified":false}]}]
```

### How to Reproduce?

- create an organization
- add an attribute `my-id` with value `1`
- `curl https://mykeycloak.com/admin/realms/myrealm/organizations\?q\=my-id:1&briefRepresentation\=true -H "Authorization: Bearer $token"
[{"id":"5d4b9102-125f-4b00-955c-472ca12b88e9","name":"realms","alias":"realms","enabled":true,"description":"","domains":[{"name":"mykeycloak.com","verified":false}]}]`

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Keycloak user attribute key broken in Keycloak 26.1.0
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/36307#issuecomment-2599759736

Following from this comment, I found a more serious problem due to the bug mentioned in the comment.

Because the keys associated with the profile change due to this bug, the user data from the token is lost because the key is wrong (for new registered users).

The first name and last name for the newly registered user are empty now.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/73ae2b26-267a-40e3-ab4c-cf16b8a6b730)

The problem is that when clicking `Save` from the `Realm settings -> User profile -> Edit attribute` page:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/16b1df9a-d98d-44e2-8456-c1754d7c8a97)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/117e9c80-ccd3-4caa-8516-18050674ba73)

The original attriute key will change automatically. The user attribute is changing from `lastName` to `profile.attributes.lastName`
 
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d4097961-08f3-4e49-8b62-7f0289fc7e1a)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/79cbdc4f-fc48-4ff0-8a7c-37b98b25ff60)


But in the mappers, the original key is used.

issue_title: Links error for https://jwt.io in documentation
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

docs

### Describe the bug

The links check in documentation are failing for `https://jwt.io`. For example in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/actions/runs/12903513066/job/35978901869

```
https://jwt.io/ (invalid status code 500)
```

The reason is that now jwt.io seems to require the `Accept-Language` header:

```bash
curl -s -o /dev/null -I -w "%{http_code}\n" https://jwt.io
500
curl -s -o /dev/null -I -w "%{http_code}\n" -H "Accept-Language: en" https://jwt.io
200
```

I'll add the workaround to send that header in the links class.

### Version

999

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

No error

### Actual behavior

https://jwt.io/ (invalid status code 500)

### How to Reproduce?

```
cd docs/documentation/
mvn clean install
```

### Anything else?

_No response_

([ref](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/docs/guides/server/fips.adoc?plain=1#L34-L35))

https://central.sonatype.com/artifact/org.bouncycastle/bctls-fips/2.0.19

etc...  
THis licence is the same as the regular non FIPS BouncyCastle library.

N/A

The docs do not tell alternative facts

### Actual behavior

The docs tell alternative facts

issue_title: SAML2 Client Signing Keys Config does not accept PEM import
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

When I want to create a new SAML2 client in Keycloak and I want to require client signatures, I can click Regenerate and receive a private key. The conceptual problem is that
a) keycloak should never know the private key
b) I don't want to transfer the private key to my client.
Private keys are private. They should be generated on the client and remain on the client. What I actually want to do is to import the client's public certificate as trusted.
This behaviour is broken again (cf. https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/36708).

When trying to work around the issue, I tried to "turn it off and on again":

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c5087f23-8af8-4e72-8963-582e1b4a4055)

When you turn "Client signature required" back on, a dialog appears, letting you choose to either generate a client cert on KC or to import an existing one:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/55d8a339-3ac3-41d8-b8f4-927754134d51)

When choosing to import a client cert in this dialog, I am again forced to upload the private key to keycloak, as "Import Certificate PEM" is missing from the drop down:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/21cf2c2c-494e-4c20-8d70-0dcba8e02c63)

This relates to an older issue: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/17514

### Version

26.1.0

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

I can import a PEM certificate (only the public part) as SAML Client Certificate

### Actual behavior

I have to choose from JKS, PKCS12, BCFKS, which are all container formats which include the private key.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create SAML Client
2. Go to "Keys"
3. Deactivate "Client signature required"
4. Reactivate "Client signature required"
5. Choose "Import" Radio Button
6. Be disappointed, that PEM is missing from the Archive Formats :)

### Anything else?

issue_title: Conflict when Keycloak uses an OpenShift cluster ingress certificate
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

operator

### Describe the bug

If a Keycloak instance is deployed using the OpenShift cluster ingress certificate , *.app.<cluster-domain> , when a client (browser) establishes a connection with Keycloak, the requests to other applications deployed on the same OpenShift cluster may be received by Keycloak instead of the requests being received by the application.

The Keycloak connection is reused and the request is received by Keycloak.

Workaround

Use reencrypt route for ingress controller

A reencrypt route fixes the issue because for re-encrypt routes, the Ingress Controller negotiates its connection to the application independently of the connection from the client.

route creation must be disabled on Keycloak CR , setting .spec.ingress.enabled: false

~~~
apiVersion: k8s.keycloak.org/v2alpha1
kind: Keycloak
metadata:
name: keycloak1
spec:
instances: 1
db:
vendor: postgres
host: postgres-db-w
usernameSecret:
name: keycloak-db-secret
key: username
passwordSecret:
name: keycloak-db-secret
key: password
http:
tlsSecret: tls-secret-wildcard
hostname:
hostname: keycloak1.apps.<domain>
ingress:
enabled: false
~~~



### Version

24

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Requests should go to the appropriate application, which may not be Keycloak.

### Actual behavior

Keycloak receives requests that are intended for other applications

### How to Reproduce?

Deploy a Keycloak instance using the OpenShift cluster ingress certificate , *.app.<cluster-domain>

### Anything else?

_No response_

Openshift conflict

Closes #36745

issue_title: Addition of crl cache is a breaking change
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

infinispan

### Describe the bug

As part of this PR: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/36294 a new local Infinispan cache with name `crl` was introduced. This change is a breaking change for users using a custom XML file as the cache will not be created and Keycloak will refuse to start with an error: `ISPN000436: Cache 'crl' has been requested, but no matching cache configuration exists` . 

This needs to be fixed before Keycloak 26.2 is released as we can't introduce breaking changes in minor releases.

This can be fixed by adding a check in Java whether the cache exists in XML and if it is not there [print warning and] create it. This should be only a temporary solution and as part of the next major release it should be removed and migration guide should contain steps how to migrate.

 

### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

There are no breaking changes in minor release

### Actual behavior

addition of an Infinispan cache is a breaking change for users using custom xml configuration

### How to Reproduce?

Run Keycloak with xml file that does not contain crl cache definition in xml file:
```
        <local-cache name="crl" simple-cache="true">
            <encoding>
                <key media-type="application/x-java-object"/>
                <value media-type="application/x-java-object"/>
            </encoding>
            <expiration lifespan="-1"/>
            <memory max-count="1000"/>
        </local-cache>
```

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Add default configuration for cache 'crl'

Fixes #36752

In pages using the `UserSelect` component to allow selecting users based on a search, there is a bug when you search for an user already selected and select it from the result list.

issue_title: OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant Issues: Token Issued After Authorization Denial and Browser Back
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

When using Keycloak's OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant, if a user denies authorization and then navigates back in the browser to authorize again, an access token is still issued despite the initial denial. This behavior contradicts the expected security flow, potentially ignoring the user's intent to deny authorization.

### Version

26.1.0

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When a user denies authorization using the OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant, navigating back in the browser and attempting to authorize again should not result in an access token being issued. The denial state should be correctly maintained, and no token should be issued.

### Actual behavior

After a user denies authorization, navigating back in the browser and authorizing again results in an access token being issued despite the initial denial. This allows tokens to be issued unintentionally based on the user's denial.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Configure a client application to use the OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant flow in Keycloak.
2. Initiate an authorization request from the device, opening the authorization screen in the browser.
3. On the authorization screen, select "Deny" to refuse authorization.
4. Click the browser's "Back" button to return to the authorization screen.
5. Select "Allow" to authorize again.
6. Observe that an access token is issued despite the initial denial.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Avoid token issuance after denied consent in the Device Grant flow

Closes #36811

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Graziano <g.graziano94@gmail.com>

issue_title: NullPointerException when registering a oid4vc CredentialBuilder provider component
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oid4vc

### Describe the bug

Hello, I'm trying OID4VC experimental feature and noticed that the `credential_configurations_supported` field returned by the `.well-known/openid-credential-issuer` endpoint is empty. After digging a bit into the code I understood that I have to activate a CredentialBuilder provider.

When calling the `POST /admin/realms/master/components` to activate the `jwt_vc` provider I get a 500 error. 

### Version

nightly

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

I expect that the API to register the provider component and completes successfully.

```
curl -X POST "http://localhost:9081/admin/realms/master/components"   
-H "Authorization: Bearer $ACCESS_TOKEN"   
-H "Content-Type: application/json"   -d '{
    "name": "vctest",
    "providerId": "jwt_vc",
    "providerType": "org.keycloak.protocol.oid4vc.issuance.credentialbuilder.CredentialBuilder"
  }'
```

### Actual behavior

The abovementioned API call returns an error:

```json
{"error":"unknown_error","error_description":"For more on this error consult the server log."}
```

### How to Reproduce?

Launch a keycloak instance with the oid4vc-vci feature enabled.

Get an access token 
```
export ACCESS_TOKEN=$(curl -X POST \                                                                                                                            "http://localhost:8080/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/token" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" \
  -d "username=admin" \
  -d "password=admin" \
  -d "grant_type=password" \
  -d "client_id=admin-cli" | jq -r .access_token)
```

Register the provider:

```
curl -X POST "http://localhost:8080/admin/realms/master/components" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $ACCESS_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "name": "my-custom-provider",
    "providerId": "jwt_vc",
    "providerType": "org.keycloak.protocol.oid4vc.issuance.credentialbuilder.CredentialBuilder"
  }'
```



### Anything else?

The error happens for all the providerIds of `CredentialBuilder`.

It seems that the reason is that the `getConfigProperties` of the `JwtCredentialBuilderFactory`, `LDCredentialBuilderFactory`, `SdJwtCredentialBuilderFactory` classes return null instead of an empty list.
commit_message:
Return empty configProperties for CredentialBuilderFactory implementations

Closes #36826

Signed-off-by: Enrico Costanzi <enrico.costanzi@staff.aruba.it>

issue_title: Documentation about ImportSynchronization mentions wrong interface UserStorageProvider
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

The JavaDoc for `org.keycloak.storage.user.ImportSynchronization` mentions that the interface should be implemented by any class that is implementing the `org.keycloak.storage.UserStorageProvider`. 
See here: 
- https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/5ab1bd2036831baea85626f17a54d462cede1658/model/storage/src/main/java/org/keycloak/storage/user/ImportSynchronization.java#L27

But the `org.keycloak.storage.managers.UserStorageSyncManager` is expecting the interface to be present at an implementation of a `org.keycloak.storage.UserStorageProviderFactory`. 
See here:
- https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/5ab1bd2036831baea85626f17a54d462cede1658/model/storage/src/main/java/org/keycloak/storage/user/ImportSynchronization.java#L27
- https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/5ab1bd2036831baea85626f17a54d462cede1658/model/storage-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/storage/managers/UserStorageSyncManager.java#L88

If the observation is correct, I can create an PR updating the JavaDoc of the `org.keycloak.storage.user.ImportSynchronization` and any mentionings in relation to the `org.keycloak.storage.UserStorageProvider`.

### Version

26.1.0

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Have the JavaDoc explain that `org.keycloak.storage.user.ImportSynchronization` needs to be implemented at an `org.keycloak.storage.UserStorageProviderFactory`.

### Actual behavior

The JavaDoc for `org.keycloak.storage.user.ImportSynchronization` wrongfully refers to the `org.keycloak.storage.UserStorageProvider`. 

### How to Reproduce?

Compare the JavaDoc with the actual implementation in the `org.keycloak.storage.managers.UserStorageSyncManager`.

Also, if the `org.keycloak.storage.user.ImportSynchronization` is implemented at an `org.keycloak.storage.UserStorageProvider` it is not possible to synchronize any users through the admin console.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
docs: update JavaDoc for ImportSynchronization

The JavaDoc for ImportSynchronization was wrongfully referencing the
UserStorageProvider instead of the UserStorageProviderFactory.

Closes #36834

Signed-off-by: arthenice <wistful.arthenice@gmail.com>

issue_title: Update FGAP v2 to not grant permissions of all users when permission is granted only for a single user
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/fine-grained-permissions

### Describe the bug

When granting permissions to `manage` a single user/resource all users/resources are granted when calling the `org.keycloak.services.resources.admin.permissions.UserPermissions#canView()` method.

Instead, we should check if the permission actually granted maps to the generic resource (`Users`) because this method is only about checking "global" permissions based on the generic resource type.

The same applies for other methods where there is no specific resource object as a parameter.

### Version

999.0.0-SNAPSHOT

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Only grant access to the generic resource (e.g.: `Users`) if the permission granted was for that resource.

### Actual behavior

Access is granted to the generic resource (e.g.: `Users`) even though the permission granted was for a specific resource.

### How to Reproduce?

* Create two users
* Create a permission that only allow access to a specific user and resource
* Check if the user not associated with the permission is not shown when listing users

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Update FGAP v2 to not grant permissions of all users when permission is granted only for a single user 

Closes #36838
